attacking_style: Initiates repeated attacks on climbs to fragment groups rather than solo for stage wins. In 2019 BinckBank Tour, used climb-based fragmentation combined with bonus second hunting to win GC without winning stages.
team_tactics: Elite domestique for GC leaders, setting infernal tempo on climbs to thin peloton (Romandie 2022) or control groups (Giro 2023). Trusted with high-pressure pacing when isolating rival leaders.
terrain_preference: Rolling terrain with repeated short climbs where sustained tempo-setting sheds weaker riders but keeps GC contenders engaged. Climbing punch exceeds expectations but lacks finishing speed.
vulnerability: Crash-prone on technical descents (2017 Lombardia, 2023 Vuelta). Recovery from breakaway efforts in Grand Tours costly—abandoned Giro 2019 day after hard break. Cannot sustain solo efforts when initiating too early on climbs.
key_patterns: Sets tempo on climbs to fragment peloton for team objectives, not personal glory (Romandie 2022, Giro 2023)
Repeatedly attacks climbs to create splits rather than single decisive move (BinckBank 2019, Oman 2017)
Uses bonus seconds tactically when lacking stage-winning speed—7 seconds across sprints won BinckBank GC
Abandonment risk in Grand Tours after hard breakaway efforts, recovery issues documented (Giro 2019)
Crash vulnerability on technical descents removes him from competition (Lombardia 2017, Vuelta 2023)
form_trajectory: neutral: Strong domestique work for Ineos in 2022-2023, but Vuelta crash exposure limits assessment. Peak as aggressive GC rider was 2019.
built_from: 2023 La Vuelta Ciclista a Espana Stage 1
